summary.summary: Adds scroll-to and brief highlight behavior for settings search results. Implements two view modifiers: .settingsHighlightScroll() and .settingsSearchTarget(label:) (Trio/Sources/Views/SettingsSearchHighlight.swift) to drive scrolling and a flash animation (file:91e53e46c098, diff:9704ad71784f). Introduces SettingsSearchHighlight (an @Observable environment object) and SearchResultTarget to carry a destination screen plus a scroll label (file:91e53e46c098, diff:9704ad71784f). Adds an optional scrollTargetLabels mapping to SettingItem and a scrollLabel computed property on FilteredSettingItem so search terms can map to the exact UI label used as the scroll target (file:a175000bf5e7, diff:9704ad71784f). SettingInputSection now applies settingsSearchTarget(label:) automatically (file:487a98fef4a4, diff:9704ad71784f). The Dynamic Settings screen is opted in (adds .settingsSearchTarget on a section and .settingsHighlightScroll() on the view) as the initial enabled destination (file:109c541e5954, diff:9704ad71784f). HomeRootView injects a SettingsSearchHighlight instance into the Settings navigation stack (file:73c1d912f026, diff:9704ad71784f). SettingsRootView now navigates with NavigationLink(value: SearchResultTarget(...)) and, in navigationDestination(for:), sets searchHighlight.highlightedSetting to trigger scroll+highlight on appear (file:b6ab495cbe6c, diff:9704ad71784f). New tests exercise the search mapping, the scrollTargetLabels, SearchResultTarget hashability, and SettingsSearchHighlight state transitions (file:061b0cea62e1, file:79a641262550, diff:9704ad71784f). Changes are recorded in the commits for the PR (commit:01d50dcb29ff4c55ccaaccc1884c3007be05073d, commit:be49d86a3315be6a419ea7e897a7d33d78735ac5, commit:620f190e268a968084e055ba5cf22ea13400efae, commit:6972577d295b50fcd427d08098343d460718b8ee).
summary.userFacingImpact: Tapping a settings search result will now navigate to the target screen, auto-scroll to the matching setting row, and briefly highlight it. The feature is implemented for Algorithm > Dynamic Settings in this change; other settings screens will be enabled in follow-up changes. Evidence of enabled behavior in this PR: Dynamic Settings and the shared SettingInputSection opt-in are present (file:109c541e5954, file:487a98fef4a4, diff:9704ad71784f).
